item.title: Today’s Strands #731 Hints and Answers for March 4, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #731 for Wednesday, March 4, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters and under do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints For Today, March 4, 2026

The Strands’ answers for Game #731 are all about vaked goods, specifically cakes. Therefore, you have to think of ingredients that you might use to make sweet birthday treats.

General Strands Hints For March 4

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: Where might you find items needed to make a cake?
  • Hint 2: An early System of A Down hit. The Archies were big on this, too.
  • Hint 3: A big hint that the cake you’re about to eat is a birthday cake

Use these clues to narrow down your guesses and increase your chances of solving Strands #731 for March 4, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for March 4, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • LULL
  • SELL
  • KING
  • CASE
  • SODA

Today’s Strands Answer For Game #731 On March 4, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • FROSTING
  • CANDLES
  • FLOUR
  • COCOA
  • VANILLA
  • SUGAR
  • SPANAGRAM: BAKINGAISLE

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #731?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on March 5 will be Strands Game #732.

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett serves as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since its launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Assassin’s Creed Unity 60 FPS Patch Coming to Xbox and PS5 Tomorrow
item.content:encoded:
Assassin’s Creed Unity is getting a 60 FPS patch on Xbox Series consoles and PlayStation 5.

Ubisoft recently announced on a new blog about the future of the franchise that Assassin’s Creed Unity, which was initially released on the PS4 and Xbox One in 2014, will receive a 60 FPS update for current-gen consoles. That update will launch tomorrow, Thursday, March 5, 2026.

“We know some of you have been waiting for it for a long time, so get ready to re‑experience 18th-century Paris and Arno’s fancy parkour moves with more smoothness on consoles! And for our Xbox players out there, we’ve got a little treat, as multiple Assassin’s Creed games will be playable for free for a limited time for Xbox Free Play Days, from April 2nd to 6th! Perfect time to try Unity in that 60 FPS beauty,” the team wrote.

The Future of Assassin’s Creed

Assassin’s Creed is one of the best-selling franchises in the gaming industry. Assassin’s Creed: Shadows was released nearly a year ago, and in its first two months, approximately 3 million copies were sold, with nearly 75% of those sales on consoles.

On March 20, Ubisoft will host a Twitch event to discuss the latest game update. However, note that updates will become less frequent as the company shifts its focus towards the future of the franchise.

That future includes the Assassin’s Creed Black Flag remake and multiple projects in development, including Assassin’s Creed Hexe, which is promised to be a darker, narrative-driven experience. Also, the team has confirmed that Assassin’s Creed codename Invictus and Assassin’s Creed Jade are both still in development. Learn more here.

Will you be playing Assassin’s Creed unity in 60 FPS? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Ubisoft Confirms Assassin’s Creed Jade Is Still in Developement
item.content:encoded:
Assassin’s Creed Jade is currently in development.

The Assassin’s Creed franchise remains one of Ubisoft’s pillars, especially now, during a tumultuous time for the company. In a new blog focusing on the future of the franchise, Ubisoft revealed that Assassin’s Creed Jade is currently in development. It was touted as one of many projects in different stages of development.

Assassin’s Creed Jade is a mobile game set in ancient China. Purple players gained access to a beta in 2022, and footage surfaced online in 2023. The most recent report on the game came in 2024, when it was revealed that it was being delayed in hopes of a 2025 release. Obviously, that didn’t happen.

Assassin’s Creed Is Focused On Bringing Back Co-Op

Of note, the mention of Assassin’s Creed Jade being a development was immediately followed by the Assassin’s Creed team reaffirming that they are looking to bring co-op play back to the franchise. They admitted that they recently pivoted away from an early project, but are taking the lessons they learned from that project and moving forward.

They also noted that Assassin’s Creed Codename Invictus is a PvP multiplayer title being developed by a dedicated team of For Honor veterans at Ubisoft Montreal. They say player feedback is at the heart of their development approach, and they continue to explore ways to bring the community into the development experience.

For more Assassin’s Creed on Insider Gaming, check out the news on Assassin’s Creed Black Flag’s remake.

Are you excited for Assassin’s Creed Jade? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.title: Apple Unveils $599 MacBook Neo Powered By iPhone’s A18 Pro Chip
item.content:encoded:
A new budget MacBook is entering the market.

One of Apple’s strengths is its ability to offer an entry point into its ecosystem for all family members at various price levels. Its latest tech announcement, the $599 MacBook Neo, aims to provide an affordable option for those who want to experience a MacBook without spending too much on “Pro” technology.

This new device will be available starting Wednesday, March 11, exactly one week from today.

iPhone Chip Powers the New MacBook Neo

The A18 Pro chip, launched in 2024 alongside the iPhone 16 Pro models, powers this new device. The marketing says it allows users to “breeze through their go-to apps and AI activities.”

There are two options for the brand-new Neo. The more affordable $599 models are available in Silver and a “Blush” shade of pink. The higher-priced “Citrus” green and Indigo models cost $699. The more expensive models offer 512 GB of storage instead of 256. Other than that, the tech specs are the same. Both models come with 8 GB of RAM, 2 USB-C ports, and a 3.5 mm headphone jack.

Regarding battery life, Apple claims users will get up to 16 hours of video streaming and 11 hours of wireless web browsing.

Learn more about the new Chromebook alternative from Apple at this link.

Would you be interested in picking up a MacBook Neo? Are you already in the Apple ecosystem, or would this be an entryway device for you? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP
